text: Exactly two reports are produced — one for forge, one for relay — each destined to that tool's own docs/feedback path.
No feedback report (and no report-shaped section) is produced for the repo's standard linter or any tool absent from the feedback-targets table; the linter may be mentioned only as session context.
Each report stamps the tool version from the digest's manifests (forge 0.7.2, relay 1.3.0) — versions are not invented or omitted.
Each report has the unified shape: a header block (date/tool/context/outcome) plus What worked, Friction, Misses, Vacuous gates, and Proposed promotions sections.
Friction, miss, and proposal entries carry severity tags from BLOCKER/HIGH/MED/LOW.
Each miss names the phase that should have caught it (e.g. forge's unresolved fixture reference attributed to a gate/check-ready phase; relay's hollow review verdict attributed to the review phase).

The relay heartbeat finding is written as extending the prior report's finding (referencing 2026-05-30-pilot-run #2) with today's new evidence — not restated as if new. (an `extends <stem>#2` reference or an unambiguous prose link to that prior proposal both qualify; restating the heartbeat ask as a brand-new finding does not).
Findings are routed by ownership: engine liveness/review issues appear in relay's report, spec-gate issues in forge's; neither report mixes in the other tool's defects.
relay's report includes the per-PR cost/token table from the digest (its binding requires a cost table for engine runs); vacuous-gate section records the hollow PR03 review.
